meanings.meaning: 以奖金为报酬的拳击手
meanings.usage: 用于指当时没有规则、以奖金为目的的拳击比赛选手
形容缺乏精细规则或技术的体育项目

meanings.meaning: 丰富多彩的，引人注目的
meanings.usage: 形容人物或事件生动、引人关注
指用于保护拳手的专用手套
指专业训练或教学的机构
形容花费无度、经常欠债的行为
指接受某位老师或教练指导的人
指在大众中被喜爱或认可的程度
指对某事的结果下钱进行赌博
因债务或其他原因被关进监狱
指缺乏金钱和基本生活条件的状态
指在比赛或竞争中最优秀的人
专门从事拳击运动的人
指因某方面成就而被广泛认可

phrases.usage: 指把科学的方法或技术引入某项运动，使其更系统化

examples.en: Mendoza brought science to the game, revolutionising prizefighting.
examples.zh: 门多萨把科学带进拳击，使当时的拳击运动发生了革命性变化。

phrases.usage: 表示某人或某事在大众中极受欢迎

examples.en: Mendoza enjoyed tremendous popularity among all classes.
examples.zh: 门多萨在各阶层中都极受欢迎。

phrases.phrase: be adored by rich and poor alike
phrases.usage: 形容受到所有社会阶层人们的喜爱

examples.en: He was adored by rich and poor alike.
examples.zh: 无论是富人还是穷人，都对他崇拜不已。

phrases.usage: 指在短时间内迅速成名

examples.en: Mendoza rose to fame swiftly after a boxing match at fourteen.
examples.zh: 门多萨在十四岁时的一场拳击赛后迅速成名。

phrases.usage: 表示从友好或支持转为敌对或反对

examples.en: Humphries turned against him after Mendoza's success.
examples.zh: 在门多萨成功后，汉弗莱斯转向反对他。

phrases.phrase: settle the argument by a fight

examples.en: The argument could only be settled by a fight.
examples.zh: 这场争论只能通过一场拳赛来解决。

phrases.phrase: bet a great deal of money on
phrases.usage: 指在某人或某事上投入大量赌注

examples.en: The public bet a great deal of money on Mendoza.
examples.zh: 公众在门多萨身上下了大额赌注。

phrases.phrase: be paid as much as £100 for a single appearance
phrases.usage: 指一次出场费高达一百英镑

examples.en: He was paid as much as £100 for a single appearance.
examples.zh: 他一次出场就能得到高达一百英镑的报酬。

phrases.phrase: be sent to prison for failing to pay his debts
phrases.usage: 因未能偿还债务而被监禁

examples.en: He was sent to prison for failing to pay his debts.
examples.zh: 他因未能偿还债务而被送进监狱。

grammar.title: It was not until ... that ...（强调时间）
grammar.definition: 用于强调某事直到特定时间点才发生。结构为“It is/was not until + 时间/事件 + that + 主语 + 动词（过去式）”。
grammar.structure: It is/was not until + 时间/事件 + that + 主语 + 动词过去式
grammar.usage: 强调某个迟来的转折点，常用于叙述关键性事件的发生时刻。

examples.en: It was not until his third match in 1790 that he finally beat Humphries.
examples.zh: 直到1790年的第三场比赛，他才终于击败了汉弗莱斯。

examples.en: It was not until 1860 that gloves were introduced.
examples.zh: 直到1860年，手套才被引入拳击。

grammar.title: Though / Although + 从句（让步状语从句）
grammar.definition: 表示“虽然/尽管”，用于引出与主句意思相反或出乎意料的情况。
grammar.structure: Though / Although + 主语 + 动词（一般过去时）, 主句
grammar.usage: 用于对比两个事实，突出主句的重要性。

examples.en: Though he was technically a prizefighter, Mendoza brought science to the sport.
examples.zh: 虽然他在技术上是个奖金拳击手，门多萨却把科学带进了这项运动。

examples.en: Although boxing was crude, it was extremely popular.
examples.zh: 尽管拳击很粗糙，却极为流行。

grammar.title: Past Simple 与 Past Perfect 的时序区分
grammar.definition: Past Simple 用于描述已完成的具体过去动作；Past Perfect 表示在另一过去动作之前已经发生的动作，构成‘过去的过去’。
grammar.structure: Past Simple: 主语 + 动词过去式 / Past Perfect: 主语 + had + 过去分词
grammar.usage: 在叙述连续发生的过去事件时，先发生的动作用过去完成时，后发生的动作用一般过去时。

examples.en: Mendoza rose to fame after he had defeated many opponents.
examples.zh: 门多萨在击败许多对手后才成名。

examples.en: He founded an academy after he had become champion.
examples.zh: 他在成为冠军后才创办了学院。

sentencePatterns.pattern: The use of X was not introduced until Y.

original.en: The use of gloves was not introduced until 1860.
original.zh: 手套的使用直到1860年才被引入。

imitations.en: The use of safety helmets was not introduced until 1990.
imitations.zh: 安全头盔的使用直到1990年才被引入。

imitations.en: The practice of wearing seat belts was not introduced until the 1970s.
imitations.zh: 佩戴安全带的做法直到20世纪70年代才被引入。

original.en: It was not until his third match in 1790 that he finally beat Humphries.
original.zh: 直到1790年的第三场比赛，他才终于击败了汉弗莱斯。

imitations.en: It was not until the 20th century that women gained the right to vote.
imitations.zh: 直到20世纪，妇女才获得投票权。

imitations.en: It was not until sunrise that the fog lifted.

original.en: Though he was technically a prizefighter, Mendoza brought science to the sport.
original.zh: 虽然他在技术上是个奖金拳击手，门多萨却把科学带进了这项运动。

imitations.en: Though she was only fifteen, she mastered three languages.
imitations.zh: 虽然她只有十五岁，她已经掌握了三门语言。

imitations.en: Though the weather was terrible, the game went on.
imitations.zh: 虽然天气很差，比赛仍在进行。

original.en: He was so extravagant that he was always in debt.
original.zh: 他太奢侈了，以至于总是负债累累。

imitations.en: She was so nervous that she could not speak.
The problem was so complex that no one could solve it.
imitations.zh: 这个问题太复杂，没人能解决。
